entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum, the crop being improved for yield and blight resistance

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA BLIGHT
entities.description: A destructive disease caused by Ascochyta rabiei affecting chickpea

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA RABIEI
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for ascochyta blight

entities.name: BLIGHT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea cultivars to resist ascochyta blight

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of chickpea measured in seed yield

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMME
entities.description: A systematic effort to develop high-yielding, blight-resistant chickpea cultivars

entities.name: ICARDA
entities.description: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas, the institution where the breeding program started

entities.name: SYRIA
entities.description: The country where ICARDA and Tel Hadya are located

entities.name: TEL HADYA
entities.description: A location in Syria used for making crosses

entities.name: TERBOL
entities.description: A location in Lebanon used for growing F1 generations

entities.name: LEBANON
entities.description: The country where Terbol is located

entities.name: PEDIGREE METHOD
entities.description: An initial breeding method used in the program

entities.name: F4-DERIVED FAMILY METHOD
entities.description: A breeding method that replaced the pedigree method

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: An agronomic trait referring to the time to reach maturity

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: An agronomic trait referring to the height of the plant

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: An agronomic trait referring to the size of the seeds

entities.name: MEDITERRANEAN REGION
entities.description: The geographical area where ascochyta blight is endemic and new cultivars were introduced

entities.name: WINTER SOWING
entities.description: The practice of sowing crops in winter, made possible by blight resistance
